examples.title: Accessing Android System Services
examples.explanation: This example demonstrates how Android apps interact with the Application Framework layer to access system services. Each service ultimately communicates through the HAL and Linux Kernel layers of the Android architecture stack.

examples.title: Using Native Libraries via JNI
examples.explanation: JNI (Java Native Interface) bridges the Application Framework and Native Libraries layers. Apps can call C/C++ code compiled into shared libraries (.so files), which is how Android provides high-performance graphics (OpenGL ES), database (SQLite), and media processing capabilities.
